verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein in the immunoglobulin superfamily, expressed specifically in sperm. In unactivated sperm it is sequestered intracellularly (in the acrosome/membrane compartment). Upon acrosome reaction (triggered by fertilization), IZUMO1 relocates to the sperm plasma membrane outer face where it is essential for sperm-egg fusion, interacting with the egg protein JUNO. Surface exposure is thus restricted to a specific cell type (sperm) and a specific activation/acrosomal-reaction state, qualifying as tissue_restricted/cell_state-induced surface presentation.
